mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) following rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that immediately suppress equity valuations over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE increases are driven by seasonal adjustments.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer confidence and credit availability, which compounds into stronger corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during an inflationary environment signals supply-side bottlenecks that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, causing immediate negative market repricing over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns in over 50% of observations during high inflation periods, or the mechanism fails to distinguish between cyclical dips and structural supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPI exceeds 3.0 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0 percent, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting expectations of aggressive future tightening that immediately depresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 65% of threshold-crossing events, or the real rate differential loses explanatory power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC drops below 0.01, or 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth after controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where GDP growth decelerates while retail sales accelerate indicates unsustainable consumer borrowing that precedes a demand collapse, triggering negative equity returns over the next month as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of historical cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when adjusted for seasonal retail spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable inflation signals healthy capacity utilization and efficient supply chains, which reduces input cost pressures and supports immediate positive equity repricing over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of triggered instances, or the mechanism breaks down when supply growth is driven solely by inventory restocking rather than final demand.
